key_insight: The paper introduces space-time Galerkin finite element methods that preserve hypocoercivity, enabling provable exponential decay to equilibrium for kinetic Fokker–Planck equations despite degenerate diffusion.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reliable long-time simulation framework for kinetic transport/diffusion systems where stability, mass conservation, and asymptotic correctness are critical.
method: Uses continuous piecewise polynomial FEM on mixed finite/infinite meshes in space.
Imposes nonconformity through C0 interior-penalty-style numerical fluxes.
Couples the spatial discretisation with arbitrary-order hp discontinuous Galerkin time stepping.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical coercivity and exponential convergence results, then states that numerical experiments validate the predictions and observed convergence behavior.
watch_item: The approach relies on specialized exponentially weighted inverse trace inequalities and semi-infinite element machinery, which may increase implementation complexity and mesh-design effort.
paper_title: Hypocoercivity-preserving space-time Galerkin methods for kinetic Fokker-Planck equations
